channel.title: Azure on Sujith Quintelier
channel.description: Recent content in Azure on Sujith Quintelier

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Evaluate feature rollouts with Azure App Configuration Scorecards
item.description:
Azure App Configuration has added scorecards in public preview. The feature uses telemetry to show how feature flag variants are performing in production so teams can assess rollout impact.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure NetApp Files cache volumes
item.description:
Azure NetApp Files cache volumes are now generally available. The feature adds cloud-based cache volumes that store only the most actively accessed data from an external origin volume.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Virtual Network updates – default limits increased for NSGs and route tables
item.description:
Azure Virtual Network now has higher default platform limits for NSGs and route tables. The defaults are 2,000 security rules per NSG, 6,000 addresses or ports per NSG rule, and 1,000 routes per route table.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Network Watcher rule impact analyser
item.description:
Azure Network Watcher rule impact analyzer is now generally available. It lets you assess the potential effect of NSG or security admin rule changes on live network traffic before applying them.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Mock runs for Azure Storage Actions – Validate before you execute
item.description:
Azure Storage Actions now supports mock runs in general availability. This lets users simulate task execution at full scale before making any changes to Azure Blob or Data Lake Storage.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Auto activation for SaaS subscriptions in Microsoft Marketplace
item.description:
Auto activation for SaaS subscriptions is now generally available in Microsoft Marketplace. When enabled, the subscription and billing cycle start as soon as the purchase flow is completed.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Application Insights Auto-instrumentation for Azure Kubernetes Service apps
item.description:
Azure Monitor Application Insights auto-instrumentation for Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) apps is now generally available. It lets you monitor applications without changing source code, using only a few configuration steps.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Front Door WebSocket
item.description:
Azure Front Door Standard and Premium now support WebSocket in general availability. The feature is enabled by default and does not require additional configuration.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Update: Microsoft Foundry built-in RBAC role naming and enhancements
item.description:
Microsoft Foundry has renamed its built-in RBAC roles to match the product branding. Several role names were updated, including Azure AI Account Owner, Azure AI Owner, and Azure AI User.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Blob Storage SDK for Rust
item.description:
Azure Blob Storage SDK for Rust is now generally available. It supports connecting to Azure Blob Storage accounts and performing container/blob operations such as upload, download, list, and management.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Support for workloads with large files in Azure NetApp Files
item.description:
Azure NetApp Files now supports regular volumes with file sizes up to 64 TiB. This is intended for workloads that use large files, including Azure VMware Solution virtual machines with large VMDK disks.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Managed Identity Support for Azure Files SMB Is now GA
item.description:
Azure Files SMB now supports Managed Identities in general availability. Applications and services can authenticate to Azure Files without storing static credentials or account keys, using Entra-issued tokens instead.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure Container Apps Express
item.description:
Azure Container Apps Express is now in public preview. It is presented as a simplified way to deploy and scale applications on Azure without making infrastructure decisions.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Monitor dashboards with Grafana in Public, Government (Fairfax) and China
item.description:
Azure Monitor dashboards with Grafana are now generally available in Azure Public, Government (Fairfax), and China. The feature brings Grafana-based dashboard creation, editing, and sharing into the Azure Portal.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Confidential computing for Azure Service Bus Premium
item.description:
Confidential computing for Azure Service Bus Premium is now generally available in Korea Central and UAE North. It lets Service Bus process messages inside hardware-based trusted execution environments (TEEs) for added data protection.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Update: 99.99% uptime for all Azure Service Bus Premium namespaces in Availability Zone regions
item.description:
Starting May 1, 2026, Azure Service Bus Premium namespaces deployed in regions with Availability Zone support will qualify for a 99.99% uptime SLA. This applies to all Premium namespaces in those regions.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Virtual Network Manager rule impact analyzer
item.description:
Azure Virtual Network Manager rule impact analyzer is now generally available. It lets you simulate the effect of security admin rules on virtual networks before deploying them.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Sentinel TI - improved pattern parsing & revoke reliability
item.description:
Sentinel TI is now generally available with two updates: improved pattern parsing accuracy and a fix for revoke actions so they apply reliably. The change is meant to improve control in pattern-based workflows.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Premium SSD v2 Disk is now available in all three available zones in Japan West
item.description:
Azure Premium SSD v2 is now generally available in all three Availability Zones in Japan West. The disk type is positioned as a next-generation general-purpose block storage option for Azure virtual machines with sub-millisecond latency.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Dl/D/E v7 Virtual Machines
item.description:
Azure has made Dl/D/E v7 virtual machines generally available. The new general-purpose and memory-optimized VMs use Intel Xeon 6 (Granite Rapids) processors and claim up to 20% better general compute performance.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure SQL update for early-May 2026
item.description:
Azure SQL added a public preview update in early May 2026 for Business Critical Azure SQL Managed Instance. The change lets you right-size memory to improve performance without overprovisioning.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Retirement: Azure Document Intelligence v3.0 API will be retired on March 30, 2029
item.description:
Azure Document Intelligence v3.0 API will be retired on March 30, 2029. Users should migrate to the latest generally available v4.0 API before that date.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Bulk Restore for Azure Virtual Machines using Azure Backup
item.description:
Azure Backup adds Bulk Restore for Azure Virtual Machines in public preview. Customers can restore up to 100 VMs in a single operation to simplify large-scale recovery.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure Cosmos DB Shell—your new command line for data workflows
item.description:
Azure Cosmos DB Shell is in public preview. It adds a command-line interface for querying, exploring, and managing Cosmos DB data, with agentic capabilities powered by Model Context Protocol (MCP).

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure NetApp Files enable backup by default
item.description:
Azure NetApp Files now enables backup by default for newly created volumes in public preview. Backups are provisioned automatically at volume creation to simplify setup and improve data protection.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure Cosmos DB spherical quantization for improved vector search
item.description:
Azure Cosmos DB now supports spherical quantization in public preview for vector search. It is intended to improve vector indexing speed while maintaining or improving recall and accuracy at scale.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Retirement: Azure Reserved Virtual Machines Instances for select VM series
item.description:
Azure will retire certain Reserved VM Instance options for select VM series starting July 1, 2026. One-year purchases and renewals will stop for listed series, and the notice also indicates broader retirement of one-year and three-year reserved instance offerings for those series.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Application Gateway for Containers managed add-on + AKS Automatic
item.description:
Azure AKS Automatic now supports the managed Application Gateway for Containers add-on in public preview. The update removes a previous limitation that prevented Application Gateway for Containers from being used with AKS Automatic.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: AVS Support for AV64 SKU on Azure Elastic SAN
item.description:
Azure Elastic SAN datastores are now generally available with AV64 SKUs for Azure VMware Solution. This adds a higher-scale, higher-performance storage option for those deployments.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Elastic SAN support for AVS Gen2 Private Cloud
item.description:
Azure Elastic SAN support is now generally available for AVS Gen2 Private Cloud. It simplifies datastore connectivity and configuration by removing the need for an ExpressRoute gateway and using a single Private Endpoint, while also improving performance.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Single Volume Snapshots on Azure Elastic SAN
item.description:
Azure Elastic SAN now supports single volume snapshots in general availability. The feature provides incremental, point-in-time backups for individual SAN volumes, storing snapshots within the SAN.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Functions durable task scheduler Consumption SKU
item.description:
The Durable Task Scheduler Consumption SKU for Azure Functions is now generally available. It supports durable workflows and AI agent orchestrations with pay-per-use pricing and no storage, capacity planning, or idle costs.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Cleanup legacy AlternateId usage to ensure continued service
item.description:
Azure identified an issue affecting Teams Phone enablement for the Multi-line (Multiple Number Assignment) feature when customer implementations use ACS AlternateId. The guidance is to clean up legacy AlternateId usage to avoid continued service impact.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Update: Egress and data transfer with Azure in the United Kingdom
item.description:
Azure now offers free egress and at-cost data transfer for customers and CSP partners in the UK when transferring data between Azure and non-Azure environments over the internet. This expands an existing Europe transfer model to the United Kingdom.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Prefix-scoped access for User Delegation SAS
item.description:
Prefix-scoped access for User Delegation SAS in Azure Blob Storage is now generally available in all Azure regions. It adds a scope between container-level and single-blob access for SAS tokens.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Generally Available: Azure Functions support for Java 25
item.description:
Azure Functions now generally supports Java 25. You can develop and deploy Java 25 apps locally to supported Azure Functions plans on Linux and Windows, including Flex Consumption.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Microsoft Azure Red Hat OpenShift is now available in Austria East
item.description:
Microsoft Azure Red Hat OpenShift is now generally available in the Austria East region. The region provides three availability zones and supports in-country data residency for customers and partners.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Microsoft Agent Framework 1.0
item.description:
Microsoft Agent Framework is now generally available at version 1.0 for .NET and Python. The release includes stable APIs, long-term support, multi-agent orchestration, multi-provider model support, and cross-runtime interoperability via A2A.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Memory in Foundry Agent Service
item.description:
Foundry Agent Service now has a preview memory feature for managed long-term memory. It also integrates natively with Microsoft Agent Framework and LangGraph, without requiring external databases to provision, scale, or secure.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Public Preview: Container Network Insights Agent
item.description:
Azure has launched a public preview of the Container Network Insights Agent for troubleshooting Kubernetes networking issues. It provides a lightweight, web-based way to inspect and correlate network signals that are otherwise spread across multiple tools.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Connect to Elastic SAN from Windows VM via VM Extension
item.description:
Azure Elastic SAN now supports connecting volumes to Windows virtual machines through the Elastic SAN VM extension in the Azure Portal. This can be done during VM deployment.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Microsoft HTTP DDoS Ruleset for Azure WAF on Azure Front Door Premium
item.description:
Microsoft has announced public preview of the HTTP DDoS Ruleset for Azure WAF on Azure Front Door Premium. The feature is intended to help protect against HTTP-layer DDoS attacks, which can bypass traditional static controls.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Cross-region IPAM pool association in Azure Virtual Network Manager
item.description:
Azure Virtual Network Manager now supports cross-region IPAM pool association in generally available form. A single IPAM pool can be associated with virtual networks across multiple Azure regions to simplify IP address space management and reduce configuration errors.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Bring your own AI Gateway in Foundry Agent Service
item.description:
Foundry Agent Service now supports bring-your-own-model scenarios. Agents can use AI models deployed outside Foundry, including models behind enterprise AI gateways such as Azure API Management.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Retirement: Prompt Flow in Azure ML and Foundry
item.description:
Prompt Flow in Microsoft Foundry and Azure Machine Learning will be retired on April 20, 2027 and replaced by Microsoft Agent Framework. The notice says the replacement is a single orchestration platform with new capabilities, but the provided excerpt does not include migration details.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure Backup for Elastic SAN
item.description:
Azure Backup now supports Elastic SAN in public preview. It can back up and restore Elastic SAN volumes to help protect against accidental deletion, ransomware, and application-related changes.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Hosted Agents in Foundry Agent Service
item.description:
Azure Foundry Agent Service now offers hosted agents in public preview. Each agent session runs in an isolated sandbox with a clean environment, no shared state, and strong compute boundaries.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Foundry Toolkit for Visual Studio Code (formerly AI Toolkit for VS Code)
item.description:
Foundry Toolkit for VS Code is now generally available. It adds VS Code support for Microsoft Agent Framework, with agent creation from templates or GitHub Copilot, local test/debug with visualization and traces, and direct deployment to Foundry.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: WireGuard In‑Transit Encryption for Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS)
item.description:
WireGuard in-transit encryption is now generally available for Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) clusters that use Azure CNI powered by Cilium and Advanced Container Networking Services (ACNS). It provides transparent node-level encryption for traffic in transit.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Retirement: Support for .NET 8 (LTS) ends on November 10, 2026—upgrade your apps to .NET 10 (LTS)
item.description:
Support for .NET 8 (LTS) ends on November 10, 2026. App Service apps will keep running, but Microsoft will stop providing security updates and customer support for .NET 8.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Cascading read replicas in Azure Database for PostgreSQL
item.description:
Cascading read replicas are now generally available in Azure Database for PostgreSQL. You can create replicas from existing replicas, with support for up to 30 replicas across two replication levels.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: CRC Protection
item.description:
Azure Elastic SAN now supports client-enabled CRC-32C checksum verification for volume connections. CRC protection can be turned on when creating a new Elastic SAN or enabled on an existing one.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Capacity Autoscaling
item.description:
Capacity Autoscaling for Elastic SAN is now generally available. It lets customers set a policy to automatically expand SAN capacity based on usage instead of overprovisioning or managing capacity manually.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Monitor for Azure Arc-enabled Kubernetes with OpenShift and Azure Red Hat OpenShift
item.description:
Azure Monitor is now generally available for Azure Arc-enabled Kubernetes clusters running OpenShift and Azure Red Hat OpenShift. It extends monitoring for health and performance across Kubernetes infrastructure and dependent applications.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure Arc adds SQL Server on Azure Virtual Machines as a migration target
item.description:
Azure Arc migration now supports SQL Server on Azure Virtual Machines as an additional migration target. Arc-enabled SQL Server instances can now be migrated either to Azure SQL Managed Instance or to SQL Server running on Azure infrastructure using the same unified migration flow.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Logical replication slot sync Status metric for Azure PostgreSQL Flexible Server
item.description:
Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server now includes a new Azure Monitor metric, logical_replication_slot_sync_status, in public preview. It reports the synchronization status of logical replication slots.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Database for PostgreSQL flexible server in Denmark East
item.description:
Azure Database for PostgreSQL flexible server is now generally available in the Denmark East Azure region. You can deploy new flexible server instances there.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Dynamic data masking with Azure Cosmos DB
item.description:
Azure Cosmos DB now generally supports dynamic data masking, a server-side policy feature that hides sensitive data from nonprivileged users. It is intended to help protect sensitive information from unauthorized access.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Migrating from virtual network-integrated to Private Endpoint–capable network configuration
item.description:
Azure Database for PostgreSQL servers deployed with virtual network integration can now be migrated to a network configuration that supports Private Endpoint connectivity. This is in public preview.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Enhanced Mirroring Azure Database for PostgreSQL in Microsoft Fabric
item.description:
Microsoft Fabric now generally supports enhanced mirroring for Azure Database for PostgreSQL. The update adds support for native PostgreSQL types like JSON and JSONB, improving scalability and operational confidence for mirrored workloads.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Premium SSD v2 for Azure Database for PostgreSQL
item.description:
Premium SSD v2 is now generally available for Azure Database for PostgreSQL flexible server. It is positioned to deliver up to 4x higher IOPS, lower latency, and improved price-performance for I/O-intensive workloads.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Monitor pipeline
item.description:
Azure Monitor pipeline is now generally available. It provides a centralized control point for telemetry ingestion and transformation for secure, high-throughput, enterprise-scale scenarios.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Generally Available: Azure NetApp Files advanced ransomware protection
item.description:
Azure NetApp Files advanced ransomware protection (ANF ARP) is now generally available. It is intended to help detect, respond to, and recover from ransomware threats on cloud volumes.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure Monitor supports native OTLP ingestion using the Azure Monitor Agent
item.description:
Azure Monitor now has public preview support for native OTLP ingestion through the Azure Monitor Agent (AMA). OpenTelemetry-instrumented applications can send telemetry directly to AMA, which exports it to Azure Monitor.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally available: Anthropic Claude Opus 4.7 on Azure Databricks
item.description:
Azure Databricks now supports Anthropic Claude Opus 4.7 via Azure Databricks AI Model Serving. The model is available generally and is positioned for complex extraction and agentic reasoning tasks.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Retirement: Azure Functions runtime v3 on Linux Consumption will stop running September 30, 2026
item.description:
Azure will enforce the retirement of Azure Functions runtime v3 for Linux Consumption–based Function Apps. The runtime was already retired on December 13, 2022, and affected apps will stop running on September 30, 2026.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Configure AKS backup using a single Azure CLI command
item.description:
Azure Backup now supports configuring backup for Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) clusters with a single Azure CLI command. This reduces the manual steps previously required to install and set up the backup extension and related components.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Retirement: Azure Kubernetes Service support for Ubuntu 22.04
item.description:
Azure Kubernetes Service will retire support for Ubuntu 22.04 on June 30, 2027. Users should migrate to Ubuntu 24.04 or later before that date to avoid service disruption.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Generally Available: User and group quota reports in Azure NetApp Files
item.description:
Azure NetApp Files now includes user and group quota reporting in preview/GA update. The feature provides visibility into quota limits and usage for NFS, SMB, and dual-protocol volumes.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Retirement: End of lift reminder of HBv2/HC-Series/NP-Series Azure Virtual Machine in Azure Batch Pool
item.description:
Azure Batch will retire support for HBv2-series, HC-series, and NP-series VMs in Batch pools on May 31, 2027. The notice applies to these VM sizes only when used in Azure Batch pools.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Encrypt Premium SSD v2 and Ultra Disks with Cross Tenant Customer Managed Keys
item.description:
Cross-tenant customer-managed keys for Premium SSD v2 and Ultra Disks are now generally available. Managed disks can now be encrypted with a CMK stored in an Azure Key Vault in a different Microsoft Entra tenant.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure Site Recovery support for Windows Azure VMs with NVMe disk controllers.
item.description:
Azure Site Recovery now supports Azure-to-Azure replication and disaster recovery for Windows Azure VMs that use NVMe-enabled Generation 2 VM families. Supported examples include Da/Ea/Fa v6-series and Ebsv5/Ebdsv5.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Announcing: Minimum billable object size for cooler storage tiers
item.description:
Azure Blob Storage and ADLS will now use a 128 KiB minimum billable object size for the cool, cold, and archive access tiers. Objects smaller than 128 KiB on those tiers will be billed as 128 KiB.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Smart Tier (Azure Blob and Data Lake Storage)
item.description:
Smart Tier for Azure Blob and Data Lake Storage is now generally available in nearly all zonal public cloud regions. The release excludes Israel Central, Qatar Central, and UAE North at launch.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure File Sync in Belgium Central, Malaysia West, and Indonesia Central
item.description:
Azure File Sync is now generally available in Belgium Central, Malaysia West, and Indonesia Central. The service supports tiering data from on-premises Windows Servers to Azure Files for hybrid scenarios and migration.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Managed identity support for graphical session recording
item.description:
Azure Bastion graphical session recording in public preview now supports managed identities for writing to storage accounts. This removes the need to use other credentials for storage access when configuring session recording.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Storage Mover now available in Azure Government (US)
item.description:
Azure Storage Mover is now generally available in Azure Government (US). It provides a fully managed way for U.S. government customers and partners to migrate large-scale file data into Azure Government cloud environments.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Monitor AKS applications with OpenTelemetry and Azure Monitor
item.description:
Azure Monitor now supports monitoring applications running on Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) with OpenTelemetry. The preview includes autoinstrumentation to deploy the Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry distribution to workloads.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Granular Encryption-in-Transit Controls for SMB and NFS on Azure Files
item.description:
Azure Files now lets you configure encryption-in-transit separately for SMB and NFS at the storage account level. This enables protocol-specific security policies instead of a single shared setting.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Retirement: Azure Managed Grafana Essential SKU will retire on March 30, 2027
item.description:
Azure Managed Grafana Essential SKU will retire on March 30, 2027. Customers are advised to move to Azure Managed Grafana Standard SKU or Azure Monitor Dashboards with Grafana before that date to avoid service disruption.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Public Preview: StandardV2 NAT Gateway as an outbound type for AKS
item.description:
AKS Public Preview adds StandardV2 NAT Gateway as an outbound type for managed and BYO VNets. It provides zone-redundant outbound connectivity, up to 100 Gbps outbound throughput, and IPv6 outbound support.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Event Grid as a destination for Stripe events now in Public Preview
item.description:
Azure Event Grid is now in public preview as a destination for Stripe events. Developers can route Stripe events into Event Grid to build event-driven architectures without managing webhook infrastructure or custom brokers.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Enhancements to maintenance notifications in Azure Service Health
item.description:
Azure Service Health now provides regional, consolidated maintenance notifications for Azure Database for PostgreSQL. Instead of separate notices for each server, customers receive one notification per region for upcoming maintenance.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: PgBouncer 1.25.1 support in Azure Database for PostgreSQL – Flexible Server
item.description:
PgBouncer 1.25.1 is now generally available in Azure Database for PostgreSQL – Flexible Server. It is offered as a built-in connection pooling feature to help manage idle and short-lived connections with low overhead.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure Files assessments are now available using Azure Migrate
item.description:
Azure Migrate now supports public preview assessments for Azure Files. It can discover on-premises SMB and NFS file shares and provide migration planning for Windows and Linux shares to Azure Files.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Observability in AKS Namespace and Workload Views
item.description:
AKS Namespace and Workload views now include observability data from Azure Monitor managed service for Prometheus. This adds cluster and workload health, troubleshooting, and resource utilization details directly in those views.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Red Hat OpenShift adds NVIDIA H100 and H200 GPU support
item.description:
Azure Red Hat OpenShift (ARO) now supports NVIDIA H100 and H200 GPU-based Azure VM SKUs. The update targets large-scale AI, machine learning, and HPC workloads on the managed OpenShift service.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Disable HTTP proxy in AKS
item.description:
Azure Kubernetes Service now supports disabling HTTP proxy settings in an existing cluster. This helps administrators remove or change proxy configuration as network requirements evolve without needing disruptive cluster changes.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Functions now supports MCP resource triggers
item.description:
Azure Functions now generally supports MCP resource triggers for MCP servers hosted on Functions. This lets developers expose resources directly, in addition to tools.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Pod CIDR expansion in AKS
item.description:
Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) now supports generally available Pod CIDR expansion for overlay-based clusters. This lets teams increase the pod IP range when clusters run out of addresses, avoiding cluster rebuilds.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Network Security Perimeter for Azure Service Bus
item.description:
Network Security Perimeter support for Azure Service Bus is now generally available. It lets you define a logical network boundary around Service Bus namespaces and other Azure PaaS resources to block unauthorized public access.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Rule impact analysis on Azure Network Watcher
item.description:
Azure Network Watcher now offers rule impact analysis in public preview. It lets you preview how security admin rules will affect your environment before applying them, including validating behavior and identifying possible conflicts.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Retirement: Python support for Azure App Service and Azure Functions on Windows will be retired on March 31, 2027
item.description:
Python apps hosted on Azure App Service on Windows and Azure Functions on Windows will be retired on March 31, 2027. After that date, app configuration and content remain, but the apps will stop running.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Retirement: AzureDnsEndpoints for Azure storage accounts will be retired March 2027
item.description:
AzureDnsZone endpoints (preview) for Azure Blob storage accounts will be retired in March 2027. Microsoft advises moving all new and existing blob storage account deployments to Standard endpoints.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Retirement: Service Fabric support for Windows Server 2022 ends on March 31, 2027 - upgrade to Windows Server 2025
item.description:
Service Fabric support for clusters running on Windows Server 2022 ends on March 31, 2027. Customers need to move to Windows Server 2025 before then to stay supported.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Retirement: Service Fabric support for Windows Server 2019 ends on March 31, 2027 - upgrade to Windows Server 2025
item.description:
Service Fabric support for clusters running on Windows Server 2019 ends on March 31, 2027. Customers need to upgrade to Windows Server 2025 by then to stay supported.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Unlock Client-Side Configuration at Scale with Azure App Configuration and Azure Front Door
item.description:
Azure App Configuration now integrates with Azure Front Door in public preview, allowing dynamic configuration to be delivered directly to client-side applications at CDN scale. The update focuses on secure distribution and new flexibility for modern app configurations.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Data Box enhances compliance with automatic Secure Erasure Certificates
item.description:
Azure Data Box now automatically provides a downloadable Secure Erasure Certificate for each completed order. The certificate verifies the device data was securely erased according to NIST 800-88 Revision 2.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In development] Public Preview: Azure NetApp Files storage with cool access enhancement
item.description:
Azure NetApp Files adds a QoS update for cool access on Premium and Ultra service levels. Throughput now adjusts automatically as data moves to cool storage, helping balance performance and cost for mixed hot and cool workloads.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Data Box now supports Azure Files Provisioned v2
item.description:
Azure Data Box now supports ingesting data into Azure Files Provisioned v2 storage accounts. Azure Files Provisioned v2 lets customers provision and pay for specific capacity, IOPS, and throughput.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Retirement: Select Azure AI Language Features
item.description:
Microsoft will retire eight Azure AI Language features on March 20, 2027. The notice lists Key Phrase Extraction, Sentiment Analysis and Opinion Mining, Custom Text Classification, Conversational Language Understanding (CLU), Custom Question Answering (CQA), and other related language features.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Retirement: External Data Import & Data Connections in Azure Machine Learning will be retired on September 30, 2026
item.description:
Azure Machine Learning is retiring the Preview features for importing data from external sources and using external Data Connections. These features will stop being available after September 30, 2026.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure DevOps March 2026 updates
item.description:
Azure DevOps March 2026 updates include the Remote MCP Server entering public preview. The release also mentions security improvements.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure Event Grid provides new capabilities for smarter, more secure event-driven architectures
item.description:
Azure Event Grid is adding Public Preview features for event-driven architectures, focused on improved interoperability, control, and security. The announced preview includes cross-tenant webhook delivery and related capabilities for managing large-scale distributed systems.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Event Grid provides new capabilities for smarter, more secure event-driven architectures
item.description:
Azure Event Grid has added several generally available capabilities to improve event-driven system management, interoperability, and control. The announced GA feature shown in the provided content is MQTT Message Ordering, which preserves in-order delivery of MQTT messages.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: User delegation SAS for Azure Tables, Azure Files, and Azure Queues
item.description:
User delegation SAS is now generally available for Azure Tables, Azure Files, and Azure Queues. It provides a more secure SAS option than account or service SAS by binding the token to the delegator.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Microsoft Azure now available from new cloud region in Denmark
item.description:
Microsoft Azure has launched a new cloud region in Denmark, called Denmark East. It provides local cloud infrastructure for Danish customers with support for data residency and low-latency access.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Retirement: Manually registered Azure VPN Clients for ​Azure Government and ​Microsoft Azure operated by 21Vianet clouds will be retired on March 31, 2029
item.description:
Manually registered Azure VPN clients for point-to-site connections using Microsoft Entra ID authentication will be retired on March 31, 2029 in Azure Government and Microsoft Azure operated by 21Vianet clouds. Users should take action to avoid disruption.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Premium SSD v2 Disk is now available in US Gov Arizona
item.description:
Azure Premium SSD v2 is now generally available in US Gov Arizona. The disk type is a next-generation block storage option for Azure VMs and is available in a region without Availability Zones.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Ephemeral OS Disk with full caching for VM/VMSS
item.description:
Azure VM and VMSS now support Ephemeral OS Disk with full caching in public preview. The OS disk image is cached entirely on local VM storage to improve OS disk performance.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Red Hat OpenShift in Indonesia Central
item.description:
Azure Red Hat OpenShift is now generally available in the Indonesia Central Azure region. This expands regional deployment options for customers running OpenShift workloads on Azure.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Retirement: Deprecation of sidecar for remote-write for Azure Monitor managed service for Prometheus
item.description:
Azure Monitor will deprecate the sidecar used for remote-write of Prometheus metrics to Azure Monitor Workspace on March 31, 2027. Microsoft recommends moving to a self-hosted configuration and notes the change is intended to improve reliability and reduce complexity.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Retirement: Azure Cosmos DB for PostgreSQL will retire on March 31, 2029
item.description:
Azure Cosmos DB for PostgreSQL will be retired on March 31, 2029. Users must migrate to Azure Database for PostgreSQL Elastic Cluster before that date.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Premium SSD v2 Disk is now available in South India
item.description:
Azure Premium SSD v2 is now generally available in South India. The region does not have Availability Zones, and the disk type provides sub-millisecond latency as a next-generation general-purpose block storage option for Azure VMs.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure SQL updates for late-March 2026
item.description:
Late-March 2026 Azure SQL updates include support for configuring built-in SQL code analysis rules and severity settings without editing project XML, and new Fabric connectivity and provisioning options. The release note text is truncated, so only the listed items can be confirmed.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Blue-green agent pool upgrade in AKS
item.description:
Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) now offers a public preview of blue-green agent pool upgrades. The feature creates a parallel node pool with the new configuration so you can validate it before moving workloads, reducing risk from in-place upgrades.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Cosmos DB Mirroring in Microsoft Fabric with private endpoints
item.description:
Azure Cosmos DB Mirroring in Microsoft Fabric now supports private endpoints and is generally available. This lets you run analytics on operational data while keeping network traffic private.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Fabric Mirroring integration for Azure Database for MySQL
item.description:
Azure Database for MySQL – Flexible Server now has public preview support for Fabric Mirroring integration. It lets you replicate operational MySQL data into Microsoft Fabric in near real time without building or maintaining ETL pipelines.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Custom time zone support for pg_cron via cron.timezone in Azure Database for PostgreSQL
item.description:
Azure Database for PostgreSQL now supports changing the cron.timezone server parameter for pg_cron. This lets scheduled jobs be evaluated and run in a specified time zone instead of the default.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Online migration now uses the pgoutput plugin
item.description:
Azure online PostgreSQL migrations now use the pgoutput plugin for generally available online (minimal-downtime) migration. The change is intended to improve reliability, performance, and compatibility with PostgreSQL logical replication.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: PostgreSQL migration service supports compatible EDB workloads into Azure Database for PostgreSQL
item.description:
Azure Database for PostgreSQL migration service now supports EDB PostgreSQL as a source, specifically EDB Postgres Extended Server. This enables migration and consolidation of compatible EDB PostgreSQL estates into Azure using secure, reliable workflows.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: PostgreSQL migration service supports for Google AlloyDB into Azure Database for PostgreSQL
item.description:
Azure Database for PostgreSQL migration service now supports Google AlloyDB as a source. This enables migration and consolidation of PostgreSQL estates from AlloyDB into Azure using secure, reliable workflows.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Public Preview: Azure SQL Managed Instance change event streaming
item.description:
Azure SQL Managed Instance now supports public preview of change event streaming (CES), which streams row-level inserts, updates, and deletes to Azure Event Hubs in near real time. Events are published as transactions commit to reduce latency.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Container network metrics filtering for AKS
item.description:
Azure Container Networking Services (ACNS) for AKS now supports container network metrics filtering in general availability. This lets operators reduce metric volume and focus on operationally relevant data.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: AI Agent for container networking troubleshooting
item.description:
Azure has a public preview of an AI Agent for container networking troubleshooting. It provides a lightweight web-based interface to help correlate logs and metrics for Kubernetes networking incidents.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: AKS managed GPU metrics in Azure Monitor
item.description:
AKS public preview adds managed GPU metrics for NVIDIA GPU-enabled node pools. The metrics are exposed automatically into managed Prometheus, giving teams GPU performance and utilization visibility alongside Kubernetes metrics.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Cross-cluster networking in Azure Kubernetes Fleet Manager
item.description:
Azure Kubernetes Fleet Manager is adding cross-cluster networking in public preview. The update targets networking across multiple Kubernetes clusters to improve global service discovery, performance, and observability in distributed microservice environments.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Container Storage v2.1.0 now with Elastic SAN integration and on demand installation
item.description:
Azure Container Storage v2.1.0 is generally available and adds Elastic SAN integration. It lets Kubernetes clusters use storage from a shared pool and supports on-demand installation.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Container network logs in AKS
item.description:
Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) container network logs are now generally available. The feature adds visibility into pod/network traffic to help diagnose Kubernetes networking issues and failures.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Application routing with meshless Istio in AKS
item.description:
Azure AKS preview introduces Application Routing with meshless Istio as a supported ingress migration path after ingress-nginx deprecation, without requiring a full service mesh. It is positioned around Kubernetes Gateway API support and Istio-based routing.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Microsoft Azure Kubernetes Application Network
item.description:
Microsoft Azure Kubernetes Application Network is in public preview. It targets Kubernetes environments spread across regions and clusters by adding application-layer networking controls and visibility beyond IP-based networking.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Monitor Prometheus community recommended alerts for Azure Arc-enabled Kubernetes
item.description:
Azure Monitor now lets you enable Prometheus recommended alerts with one click in the Azure Portal for Azure Arc-enabled Kubernetes clusters. The alerts are based on enhanced Prometheus community rules and expand monitoring coverage for cluster scenarios.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Ingest OTLP data into Azure Monitor with the OpenTelemetry Collector
item.description:
Azure Monitor now has public preview support for native OTLP ingestion. You can send telemetry from OpenTelemetry-instrumented apps and platforms directly to Azure Monitor using the OpenTelemetry Collector.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure SQL updates for mid-March 2026
item.description:
Azure SQL preview updates in mid-March 2026 add changes aimed at reducing storage, I/O, and memory use while improving performance without requiring index maintenance jobs. The provided note is truncated and does not include the full list of enhancements.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Retirement: Azure SQL Elastic query - Shard_Map_Manager mode
item.description:
Azure SQL Elastic Query using the Shard_Map_Manager external data source type reaches end of support on March 31, 2027. Existing workloads can keep running, but this mode will no longer receive updates or support.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Retirement: Azure Sphere will be retired on July 31, 2031
item.description:
Azure Sphere will be retired on July 31, 2031. After that date, Microsoft will stop providing customer application, OS, bug, and security updates, as well as DAA certificate issuance.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure Databricks OneLake Catalog Federation
item.description:
Azure Databricks now supports OneLake catalog federation in public preview. It lets Unity Catalog query Microsoft Fabric OneLake data without copying it, while automatically syncing schemas and tables from the connected OneLake catalog.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Retirement: HBv2-series Azure Virtual Machines will be retired on May 31, 2027
item.description:
Microsoft Azure will retire HBv2-series VM sizes on May 31, 2027. The affected sizes are Standard_HB120rs_v2, Standard_HB120-96rs_v2, Standard_HB120-64rs_v2, Standard_HB120-32rs_v2, and Standard_HB120-16rs_v2.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Retirement: HC-series Azure Virtual Machines will be retired on May 31, 2027
item.description:
Microsoft Azure will retire HC-series VM sizes Standard_HC44rs, Standard_HC44-16rs, and Standard_HC44-32rs on May 31, 2027. Users should plan to migrate to a current supported VM size before the retirement date.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Retirement: NP-series Azure Virtual Machines will be retired on May 31, 2027
item.description:
Microsoft Azure will retire NP-series VM sizes Standard_NP10s, Standard_NP20s, and Standard_NP40s on May 31, 2027. Users should migrate workloads to alternative Azure GPU VM sizes before that date to avoid service disruption.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Retirement: Emissions Impact Dashboard for Azure
item.description:
The Emissions Impact Dashboard for Azure, hosted by Power BI, will be retired on March 31, 2027. After that date, it will no longer be accessible and technical support will end.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] General availability: Azure SQL updates for mid-March 2026
item.description:
Azure SQL received mid-March 2026 updates including direct publishing of SQL database projects from Visual Studio Code and the ability to view and edit SQL table data directly in MSSQ.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Red Hat OpenShift Managed Identity and Workload Identity
item.description:
Azure Red Hat OpenShift now generally supports managed identities and workload identities. This lets clusters and applications run on Azure without long-lived service principal credentials.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Database DevOps in SSMS powered by SQL projects
item.description:
SQL Database Projects are now in public preview in SSMS, bringing schema-as-code workflows for database development. The preview supports source-controlled schemas, repeatable deployments, and code quality checks in the development process.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public preview: GitHub Copilot integration in Schema Designer for the MSSQL extension
item.description:
The MSSQL extension for Visual Studio Code adds a public preview of GitHub Copilot integration in Schema Designer. This expands the visual schema design workflow with AI-assisted help based on developer feedback.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: SQL MCP Server
item.description:
Azure announced a public preview of SQL MCP Server, an MCP connector for production databases. It is part of Data API Builder and is intended to provide a simple, predictable, and secure way to connect AI agents to data workflows.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: GitHub Copilot in SQL Server Management Studio 22
item.description:
GitHub Copilot is now generally available in SQL Server Management Studio (SSMS) 22. It adds AI-powered assistance inside SSMS, including natural-language help to develop, explain, and fix SQL-related tasks.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Data API builder with built-in GitHub Copilot in MSSQL extension
item.description:
The MSSQL extension for Visual Studio Code adds Data API builder in public preview with built-in GitHub Copilot integration. It provides a guided experience for generating backend APIs directly in the development workflow.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Lakeflow Connect Free Tier now available in Azure Databricks
item.description:
Azure Databricks has launched a Lakeflow Connect Free Tier in Azure Databricks. Each workspace gets 100 free DBUs per day for ingestion from SaaS applications and databases, which is described as supporting roughly 100 million records per workspace per day.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Versionless key support for transparent data encryption in Azure SQL Database
item.description:
Azure SQL Database now supports versionless keys for transparent data encryption (TDE). This removes the need to reference a specific key version in Azure Key Vault or Managed HSM, simplifying customer-managed key setup and reducing maintenance.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Default Rule Set 2.2 and updates to ruleset support policy
item.description:
Azure WAF has made Default Rule Set (DRS) 2.2 generally available on Azure Application Gateway and Azure Front Door. The managed ruleset support policy is being updated to reflect this release and provide more predictable support guidance.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Foundry Agent Service
item.description:
Microsoft announced general availability of the next-generation Foundry Agent Service. It introduces a redesigned API format and runtime experience for building and operating agents from prototype to production.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Retirement: Azure VMware Solution AV36P and AV52 node retirement on June 30, 2029
item.description:
Microsoft will retire Azure VMware Solution AV36P and AV52 nodes on June 30, 2029. Existing AV36P and AV52 Reserved Instance terms are not affected, but customers should review RI expirations and plan migration before the retirement date.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Retirement: Migration of Azure Batch Low-Priority VMs to Spot VMs in early March
item.description:
Azure Batch will begin a system-initiated migration from Low-Priority VMs to Spot VMs starting March 1, 2026. Low-Priority VMs were retired on September 30, 2025, and the change aligns Batch with Azure Spot infrastructure.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Retirement: Support for Windows Server 2016 Marketplace images on Azure Batch pools will end on January 12, 2027
item.description:
Azure Batch will stop supporting Windows Server 2016 Marketplace images on Batch pools on January 12, 2027. This follows the end of extended support for Windows Server 2016.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Entra ID-Based Access for Azure Blob Storage SFTP
item.description:
Microsoft Entra ID-based access for Azure Blob Storage SFTP is now in public preview. It lets you use Entra ID identities, including guest users through Entra External Identities, to connect securely to Azure Blob Storage via SFTP.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Retirement: End of life reminder of NVv3 and NVv4-series Azure virtual machine in Azure Batch pools
item.description:
Microsoft Azure will end support for NVv3 and NVv4-series virtual machines in Azure Batch pools on September 30, 2026. The notice lists affected NVv3 sizes, including Standard_NV12s_v3, NV12hs_v3, NV24s_v3, NV24ms_v3, NV32ms_v3, and NV48s_v3.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Retirement: Flatcar Container Linux for AKS (preview)
item.description:
Azure Kubernetes Service support for Flatcar Container Linux for AKS in preview is being retired on June 8, 2026. Users should move to a supported alternative by that date; the preview remains usable until June 7, 2026.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure Storage Mover enables private data transfers from AWS S3 to Azure Blob
item.description:
Azure Storage Mover is now in public preview for private transfers from AWS S3 in a Virtual Private Cloud to Azure Blob Storage. It supports secure private networking and automation, reducing the need for manual pipelines or third-party tools.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Summary rules in Log Analytics workspace now support manual “Retry bin”
item.description:
Log Analytics workspace summary rules now support a manual “Retry bin” action in generally available release. This lets you rerun failed bins to fill gaps in summarized data.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Customer‑managed encryption keys now supported on Premium SSD v2 disks for Azure Database for PostgreSQL
item.description:
Azure Database for PostgreSQL now supports customer-managed keys (CMKs) with Premium SSD v2 disks in public preview. This lets you control encryption of data at rest for workloads using these disks.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Database for PostgreSQL dashboards with Grafana
item.description:
Azure Database for PostgreSQL now has built-in Grafana dashboards in the Azure portal. The integration uses Azure Monitor, so you do not need a separate Grafana instance to view metrics.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Terraform, Bicep, Ansible support for elastic clusters on Azure Database for PostgreSQL
item.description:
Azure Database for PostgreSQL elastic clusters now have General Availability support for provisioning and management through Terraform, Bicep, and Ansible. This adds native infrastructure-as-code options for creating, scaling, and managing elastic clusters on Azure.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Query Profiler in MSSQL extension for Visual Studio Code
item.description:
The MSSQL extension for Visual Studio Code now has Query Profiler in public preview. It lets you capture query and database activity and view it in real time within VS Code.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure SRE Agent with new capabilities
item.description:
Azure SRE Agent is now generally available. The GA release adds new capabilities focused on incident diagnosis, automated response workflows, and improving uptime while reducing operational toil.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Retirement: Azure Policy faster enforcement and retirement of login/logout workaround
item.description:
Azure Policy now enforces new and updated Resource Manager mode policy assignments within 5 minutes. As a result, the previously recommended login/logout workaround (used to refresh cached policy state) is being retired.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Draft & Deploy on Azure Firewall
item.description:
Azure Firewall Policy adds a Draft & Deploy workflow that separates editing from deployment to reduce deployment time and operational disruption when updating policies.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Databricks Lakebase
item.description:
Azure Databricks Lakebase is now generally available as a managed PostgreSQL service designed for OLTP with separated storage and compute.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Databricks update workspace network configuration
item.description:
Azure Databricks now generally supports updating an existing workspace’s network configuration.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally available: Azure Premium SSD v2 Disk Storage is now available in a third Availability Zone in New Zealan North
item.description:
Azure Premium SSD v2 Disk Storage is now available in a third Availability Zone in the New Zealand North region.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: DCesv6, DCedsv6, ECesv6, and ECedsv6 confidential VMs
item.description:
Azure announced general availability of the DCesv6, DCedsv6, ECesv6, and ECedsv6 confidential VM series based on 5th Gen Intel Xeon processors with Intel Trust Domain Extensions (TDX).

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Retirement: Managed NGINX Ingress with Application Routing Add-on Retiring November 2026
item.description:
Microsoft will retire the Managed NGINX Ingress used by the AKS Application Routing add-on in November 2026 due to the upstream Ingress-NGINX project deprecating and ending updates after March 2026. Microsoft will provide only critical security patches until 30 Nov 2026, after which the controller is unsupported.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Red Hat OpenShift is now available in Malaysia West, New Zealand North, and Mexico Central
item.description:
Azure Red Hat OpenShift (ARO) is now generally available in three additional Azure regions: Malaysia West, New Zealand North, and Mexico Central.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Restrict usage of user delegation SAS to an Entra ID identity
item.description:
Azure Storage adds a preview feature to restrict (bind) user delegation SAS usage to a specific Microsoft Entra ID identity, strengthening authentication by tying SAS access to the user.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Secure ingestion and pod placement for Azure Monitor pipeline
item.description:
Azure Monitor pipeline adds public-preview support for secure ingestion from external endpoints using TLS/mTLS and Bring Your Own Certificates (BYOC), plus new controls for pod placement.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure SQL updates for late-February 2026
item.description:
Late-February 2026 Azure SQL updates add a database object search feature to the MSSQL extension for Visual Studio Code.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure SQL updates for late-February 2026
item.description:
Late-February 2026 preview updates for Azure SQL add in-dialog database management actions and introduce flat-file import support in the MSSQL extension.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Geo‑redundant backups for Premium SSD v2 in Azure Database for PostgreSQL
item.description:
Azure Database for PostgreSQL now supports configuring geo-redundant automated backups when using Premium SSD v2 disks (public preview). This adds a disaster-recovery option by storing backups in a geo-redundant manner.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Retirement: Windows Server Annual Channel (preview)
item.description:
Windows Server Annual Channel (Preview) on AKS will retire on May 15, 2026. Users should migrate to Windows Server Long Term Servicing Channel (LTSC) before that date.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: WAF Insights for Application Gateway
item.description:
Azure Application Gateway WAF Insights is now in public preview, adding an interactive view of WAF logs and metrics. It is intended to help investigate blocked requests and analyze attack patterns.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Premium SSD v2 Disk is now available in Brazil Southeast, and in a third Availability Zone in Malaysia West and Indonesia Central
item.description:
Azure Premium SSD v2 disks are now generally available in Brazil Southeast and expanded to a third Availability Zone in Malaysia West and Indonesia Central.

  • Source: Azure Updates
